up.method: HPLC (Dionex 2000, Rezex ROA-Organic Acid H+); OD620->CDW via CDW=0.75xOD; averaged over 2 replicates

sec.method: HPLC; secretion rate increased during growth phase; lactate secreted first
HPLC; secretion followed lactate; increased strongly during growth phase for DSM 4252T

growth_records.cite: Thordis Kristjansdottir et al. A genome-scale metabolic reconstruction provides insight into the metabolism of the thermophilic bacterium Rhodothermus marinus. FEMS Microbiology Ecology (2025). DOI:10.1093/femsec/fiae167. [PMC11730185]
growth_records.snippet: Experimental growth data was obtained for strains DSM 4252T and ISCaR-493 in bioreactors, along with measured uptake of glucose and pyruvate and secretion of acetate and lactate (Supplementary File 1). The average rates of two replicates wa
growth_records.notes: GEM validation paper. Measured EXPERIMENTAL rates (glucose+pyruvate uptake, lactate+acetate secretion) and specific growth rate were obtained from duplicate bioreactor batch cultivations and used to constrain the Rmarinus_578 model; the model 'correctly predicted the growth rates'. The actual NUMERIC rate values reside only in Supplementary File 1 (a zip not present in the article XML text); text reports the conditions and methods but no numeric mu/qS/qP values, so rate fields left null. DSM 4252T did NOT show a true exponential phase (apparent specific growth rate decreased over time); data from time points 3-6 used for constraining. CDW conversion: CDW(g/l)=0.75xOD620.

up.method: complete glucose consumption after 14 h (derived average)

yields.note: EPS productivity 0.071 +/- 0.001 mg/L/h; CDW 1.2 +/- 0.14 mg/L (as printed)

growth_records.cite: Mukti IJ et al. Medium development and production of carotenoids and exopolysaccharides by the extremophile Rhodothermus marinus DSM16675 in glucose-based defined media. Microbial Cell Factories (2022). DOI:10.1186/s12934-022-01946-7. [PMC9590192]
growth_records.snippet: Cultivation in DMB medium with 2 g/L glucose resulted in repeatable growth, a maximum cell density (OD620 nm) of 2.36 +/- 0.057 (1.2 +/- 0.14 mg/L CDW) after 14 h cultivation, a maximum specific growth rate (mu max) of 0.22 h-1 and complete
growth_records.notes: Aerobic thermophile, 65 C, 200 rpm, baffled shake flask, pH 7.2. mu_max 0.22 /h reported; doubling time calculated = ln2/0.22 = 0.693/0.22 = 3.15 h. Glucose (2 g/L) fully consumed by 14 h -> derived average uptake 2/14 = 0.143 g/L/h (no per-time rate reported for DMB; CDW as printed is 1.2 mg/L, implausibly low, likely g/L typo - reported verbatim). EPS 1.72+/-0.03 mg/L (24 h), carotenoids 0.59e-3 mg/L (18 h). EPS yield 0.75 mg/g, productivity 0.071 mg/L/h (Table 2).

growth_records.medium: Modified Wolfe's medium + 0.05 g/L yeast extract + 2.5 g/L glucose

up.units: g/L consumed over 36 h (total; ~0.067 g/L/h average)

yields.note: EPS productivity 0.040 +/- 0.01 mg/L/h; CDW 0.95 +/- 0.07 mg/L (as printed)

growth_records.cite: Mukti IJ et al. Medium development and production of carotenoids and exopolysaccharides by the extremophile Rhodothermus marinus DSM16675 in glucose-based defined media. Microbial Cell Factories (2022). DOI:10.1186/s12934-022-01946-7. [PMC9590192]
growth_records.snippet: a similar specific growth rate when yeast extract supplementation was used (mu max of 0.24 h-1)... final OD620 nm of 2.07 +/- 0.05 (1.05 +/- 0.07 mg/L CDW) after 22 h with yeast extract
growth_records.notes: Modified Wolfe's medium WITH 0.05 g/L yeast extract, 2.5 g/L glucose, 65 C, 200 rpm, pH 7.2. mu_max 0.24 /h reported; doubling time = ln2/0.24 = 2.89 h. Consumed glucose 2.42 g/L over 36 h (Table 2); derived average uptake ~0.067 g/L/h. EPS 1.74+/-0.20 mg/L (36 h), yield 0.63 mg/g, productivity 0.040 mg/L/h. Note yeast extract makes medium not fully defined.

growth_records.medium: Modified Wolfe's medium (no yeast extract) + 2.5 g/L glucose

up.units: g/L consumed over 30 h (total; ~0.079 g/L/h average)

yields.note: EPS productivity 0.043 +/- 0.004 mg/L/h; CDW 1.2 +/- 0.14 mg/L (as printed)

growth_records.cite: Mukti IJ et al. Medium development and production of carotenoids and exopolysaccharides by the extremophile Rhodothermus marinus DSM16675 in glucose-based defined media. Microbial Cell Factories (2022). DOI:10.1186/s12934-022-01946-7. [PMC9590192]
growth_records.snippet: it decreased to a mu max of 0.17 h-1 and 0.14 h-1 respectively, in the cultivations without this supplementation... 2.35 +/- 0.29 (1.2 +/- 0.14 mg/L CDW) after 30 h without yeast extract
growth_records.notes: Modified Wolfe's medium WITHOUT yeast extract (fully defined), 2.5 g/L glucose, 65 C, 200 rpm, pH 7.2. mu_max 0.17 /h reported; doubling time = ln2/0.17 = 4.08 h. Consumed glucose 2.37 g/L over 30 h (Table 2); derived avg uptake ~0.079 g/L/h. EPS 1.32+/-0.09 mg/L, yield 0.57 mg/g.

growth_records.medium: Modified Wolfe's medium (no yeast extract) + 5 g/L glucose

up.units: g/L consumed over 36 h (total; ~0.049 g/L/h average; incomplete utilization)

yields.note: EPS productivity 0.040 +/- 0.002 mg/L/h; CDW 1.15 +/- 0.07 mg/L (as printed)

growth_records.cite: Mukti IJ et al. Medium development and production of carotenoids and exopolysaccharides by the extremophile Rhodothermus marinus DSM16675 in glucose-based defined media. Microbial Cell Factories (2022). DOI:10.1186/s12934-022-01946-7. [PMC9590192]
growth_records.snippet: mu max of 0.17 h-1 and 0.14 h-1 respectively, in the cultivations without this supplementation... 2.88 +/- 0.13 (1.35 +/- 0.07 mg/L CDW) after 36 h without yeast extract
growth_records.notes: Modified Wolfe's, no yeast extract, 5 g/L glucose. mu_max 0.14 /h reported; doubling time = ln2/0.14 = 4.95 h. Only 1.76 g/L of 5 g/L glucose consumed in 36 h (incomplete, substrate inhibition at high conc); derived avg uptake ~0.049 g/L/h. EPS 1.45+/-0.1 mg/L, yield 0.82 mg/g. Text notes glucose consumed primarily for EPS not growth at 5 g/L.

growth_records.medium: Defined minimal R. marinus medium (DRM) + up to 5 g/L glucose

up.units: g/L/h (max, DMB-P without ferric citrate; up to 5 g/L glucose)

yields.note: CDW 2.85 +/- 0.07 mg/L (as printed); almost doubled carotenoids and EPS vs original media

growth_records.cite: Mukti IJ et al. Medium development and production of carotenoids and exopolysaccharides by the extremophile Rhodothermus marinus DSM16675 in glucose-based defined media. Microbial Cell Factories (2022). DOI:10.1186/s12934-022-01946-7. [PMC9590192]
growth_records.snippet: The resulting defined minimal R. marinus medium, (DRM), allowed reproducible cultivations to a final OD620nm of 6.6 +/- 0.05, CDW 2.85 +/- 0.07 mg/L, a maximum specific growth rate (mu max) of 0.26 h-1, total carotenoids 0.77 x 10-3 mg/L an
growth_records.notes: Final optimised defined minimal medium DRM (DMB with high phosphate + Wolfe's trace/vitamins), up to 5 g/L glucose, 65 C, 200 rpm, pH 7.2. mu_max 0.26 /h reported; doubling time = ln2/0.26 = 2.67 h. Highest OD620 6.6, EPS 3.4 mg/L, carotenoids 0.77e-3 mg/L. Glucose max consumption rate from the DMB-P screening step: 0.43 g/L/h with ferric citrate + amino acids, 0.48 g/L/h without ferric citrate (final OD 5.26 and 5.86 resp.); 0.36-0.39 g/L/h without amino acids. Full DRM salt table split across Tables 1,3,4,5.

derived.qs_mu_note: pooled across studies/media — not a single chemostat series, so no maintenance model fitted
